tree.empty_group: Group is empty — add a child or remove the group.

validation.port: Not a valid port or port range.

settings.title: Settings
settings.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
settings.env_subtitle: Runtime configuration is read-only — edit your compose / env file and restart.
settings.invariants_subtitle: Custom lint rules on top of the universal catalogue. Stored in {path}.
settings.invariants_active_guardrail: Disabling an invariant silences its violations in the lint run. Use with care — the check will not fire again until re-enabled.
settings.invariants_parse_errors: {count} entry had a schema error and was dropped. | {count} entries had schema errors and were dropped.
settings.invariants_form_id_hint: Letters, digits, dot, underscore, hyphen. First char alphanumeric.
settings.invariants_form_rule_required_values: At least one value is required.
settings.invariants_form_id_bad_chars: ID must match the pattern: letters, digits, dot, underscore, hyphen.
settings.invariants_form_id_duplicate: Another invariant already uses this ID.

onboarding.title: Welcome to Miharbor
onboarding.subtitle: Mihomo config file not found at {path}
onboarding.question: Would you like to create a minimal starter config?
onboarding.about_seed: The starter config enables TUN + fake-ip DNS with a single "Default → DIRECT" service. Your new mihomo API secret is auto-generated (64 hex chars).

deploy_live.health_canonicalized_banner: Config was canonicalized on startup — see History

providers.title: Rule providers
providers.subtitle: External rule-sets (adblock lists, GeoSite, inline rules) referenced from `rules:` as `RULE-SET,<name>,…`.

list.title: Configured providers
list.empty: No rule providers configured yet. Use "Add provider" to define one.

form.name: Name
form.name_taken: A provider with this name already exists.
form.interval_hint: How often mihomo automatically re-fetches the ruleset. Manual refresh is always available.
form.proxy_hint: Optional: which proxy group mihomo routes the fetch through (for geo-blocked sources). Leave empty to fetch directly.
form.payload_hint: One rule body per line — e.g. "DOMAIN-SUFFIX,example.com", "IP-CIDR,10.0.0.0/8,no-resolve". Do NOT include the "RULE-SET,NAME," prefix (mihomo adds it automatically).
form.err_url_required: URL is required for http providers.
form.err_interval_required: A refresh interval greater than 0 is required for http providers.
form.err_path_required: Path is required for file providers.
form.err_payload_required: Inline providers need at least one rule.

extras.title: Preserved entries
extras.note: These entries couldn't be fully parsed but are preserved verbatim in the YAML — edit them in Raw YAML.

dns.title: DNS
dns.subtitle: Mihomo DNS engine — fake-ip, resolvers, domain policies.
dns.save_failed: Couldn't save DNS changes — check the YAML in Raw YAML.

fields.listen_hint: Client-facing DNS is usually AdGuardHome/dnsmasq; mihomo listens on the loopback only.

guardrails.listen_lan: Binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes mihomo DNS on every interface.
guardrails.listen_port_53: Port 53 usually collides with AdGuardHome/systemd-resolved — use 1053 or another high port.
guardrails.literal_ip_required: This field MUST use literal IPs. Hostname-based resolvers cause DNS loops because mihomo can't resolve them before DNS is up.
guardrails.listen_header: Changing this alters the whole DNS chain on the host.
guardrails.proxy_ns_header: Resolves proxy server hostnames BEFORE DNS is up — literal IPs only.
guardrails.default_ns_header: Bootstrap resolvers used to look up DoH hostnames — literal IPs only.

nameserver_policy.title: Nameserver policy
nameserver_policy.description: Route specific domains to specific resolvers. Rows are evaluated top-down; first match wins.

extras.note: Unknown fields preserved verbatim from the YAML — edit them in Raw YAML.

tun.title: TUN
tun.subtitle: Mihomo TUN interface — transparent interception + routing.

fields.device_hint: The TUN interface name shown by `ip link`. Empty → mihomo picks an OS-specific default (utun* / tun*).
fields.stack_hint: `system` uses the OS TCP/IP stack (fastest). `gvisor` provides a user-space sandbox for hostile environments. `mixed` combines both.
fields.interface_name_hint: Explicit WAN interface — preferred over auto-detect on multi-NIC hosts.

guardrails.device_empty: Device name is unset — mihomo will pick an OS-specific default (utun* on macOS, tun* on Linux). Set an explicit name (e.g. `mihomo-tun`) for easier debugging.
guardrails.auto_detect: `auto-detect-interface` usually works, but breaks on dual-NIC hosts (e.g. routers with WAN≠LAN). Prefer setting `interface-name` explicitly.

dns_hijack.hint: List of host:port entries TUN intercepts for DNS. Leave empty to disable DNS takeover (recommended on first rollout).

route_exclude.description: CIDRs that bypass TUN. Must include every proxy-server IP — without this, mihomo's own proxy traffic loops back through its TUN and the tunnel dies.
route_exclude.empty: No exclusions yet — add every proxy-server IP as a /32.
route_exclude.missing_hint: Add these IPs to the exclusion list (as /32) so mihomo's proxy traffic doesn't recurse through its own TUN.

route_address.hint: Additional CIDRs to route through TUN. Most setups don't need this — `auto-route: true` handles the defaults.

extras.note: Unknown fields preserved verbatim from the YAML — edit them in Raw YAML.

sniffer.title: Sniffer
sniffer.subtitle: Mihomo protocol sniffer — HTTP/TLS/QUIC destination inference.

fields.override_destination: Override destination (rewrite rule-match target to sniffed domain)
fields.force_dns_mapping: Inject sniffed hostname into DNS cache
fields.force_domain_hint: Domains that always use the sniffed hostname, even when override-destination is off.
fields.port_whitelist_hint: If non-empty, ONLY these ports are ever sniffed — per-protocol configs are overridden. One range per entry (e.g. "80", "8080-8090").

guardrails.override_destination: `override-destination` rewrites the rule-matching destination from IP to the sniffed domain. It's useful but surprising — domain-based rules will match traffic that CIDR rules would otherwise catch. Understand the consequences before enabling.

protocols.description: Configure ports mihomo should monitor per protocol. Each entry is a single port ("80") or an inclusive range ("80-90"). Comma-separated lists are NOT valid — use multiple entries.
protocols.empty: No port ranges — this protocol is not sniffed.
protocols.extras_note: Preserved verbatim from the YAML — edit in Raw YAML.

extras.note: Unknown fields preserved verbatim from the YAML — edit them in Raw YAML.

profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Top-level mihomo config — mode, log-level, external-controller, secret, and related knobs.

fields.external_controller_hint: host:port for mihomo's control API. Bind to 127.0.0.1 when possible; LAN/WAN binds REQUIRE a secret.
fields.geo_update_interval_hint: How often mihomo refreshes the GeoIP / GeoSite databases.

nested.description: mihomo's nested `profile:` map. These are persisted runtime preferences, not routing policy.

auth.description: HTTP proxy authentication entries (user:pass). Usernames are shown below; passwords are write-only — edit a row to set a new password, but previously-saved passwords are never echoed back in the UI.
auth.dialog_description: Passwords are written directly into the mihomo config. Keep a copy of them in your own password manager — the UI will not display them again after save.
auth.dialog_guardrail: Passwords are never echoed back in the UI after save. If you forget one, remove the entry and re-add the user.
auth.user_taken: A user with this name already exists.

guardrails.ipv6: IPv6 is enabled. The runbook keeps IPv6 disabled until extended burn-in — the NAT / rule plane has not been stressed for IPv6 yet.
guardrails.external_controller: External controller is bound to a non-loopback address without a secret. Any LAN host can control mihomo. Set a 64-char hex secret.
guardrails.secret: The secret is the Bearer token for mihomo's control API. Treat it like a password: never commit it, rotate after suspected leaks (`openssl rand -hex 32`, then restart mihomo and re-enter in your dashboard).
guardrails.authentication: Passwords are written verbatim to the mihomo config file. After save, the UI only shows usernames — if you forget a password, you must remove the entry and re-add the user.

extras.note: Unknown fields preserved verbatim from the YAML — edit them in Raw YAML.

suggestions.unreachable_rule_after_match: This rule can never execute because a MATCH rule above it will catch all traffic. Either remove this rule or move it before the MATCH rule at #{covered_by_index}.
suggestions.unreachable_rule_shadowed: This rule is shadowed by rule #{covered_by_index} which covers a strictly wider set of inputs. Either remove this rule or reorder them.
suggestions.duplicate_rule: This rule is a duplicate of rule #{duplicate_of_index}. Remove one of them.
suggestions.dangling_group_reference: The target proxy-group '{target}' does not exist. Create it in the proxy-groups section, or change the target to an existing group or DIRECT/REJECT.
suggestions.dangling_node_reference: The proxy node '{ref}' does not exist in the proxies section or as another group. Add it to the proxies section, or remove the reference from group '{group}'.
suggestions.dangling_ruleset_reference: The rule-provider '{provider}' does not exist. Add it to the rule-providers section, or change the rule to reference an existing provider.
suggestions.intra_group_duplicate: This rule is a duplicate of rule #{duplicate_of_index} (same type and value, both targeting '{group}'). Remove one of them.
suggestions.cross_group_duplicate: This rule has the same type and value as rule #{firstAt}, but targets a different group ('{currentTarget}' vs '{firstTarget}'). This is sometimes intentional but often indicates an inconsistency.
suggestions.rule_parse_error: Could not parse the rules section. The error was: {message}. Check the YAML syntax — rules should be a list of items in the format TYPE,VALUE,TARGET[,modifiers].
suggestions.universal_invariant: This configuration violates a built-in invariant (id: {id}). Review the config requirements in your runbook or admin guide. For details, see the main error message.
suggestions.user_invariant: This violates a custom invariant: {name}. {description}. Review your invariants configuration and make the necessary changes.
